tierDefinitions.runtime_projection: Meta_Kim has a formal repo projection, runtime profile, projection layout, sync manifest entry, and validation coverage.
tierDefinitions.dependency_install_target: A dependency installer can target the product, but Meta_Kim does not own a formal runtime projection for it.
tierDefinitions.candidate_probe: External docs or user demand suggest compatibility, but Meta_Kim needs a probe, adapter design, and validation before promotion.

surfaceTaxonomy.instruction_context: Persistent project or global instructions such as AGENTS.md, rules, steering, memories, or custom instruction files.
surfaceTaxonomy.skill_workflow: Portable procedure bundles such as SKILL.md folders, skills, workflows, powers, or equivalent reusable playbooks.
surfaceTaxonomy.agent_mode: Named agents, subagents, custom modes, assistants, or role-specific execution contexts.
surfaceTaxonomy.hook_automation: Pre/post tool hooks, prompt hooks, lifecycle hooks, file event automation, or workflow automation triggers.
surfaceTaxonomy.mcp_tooling: MCP server configuration, MCP tools, or compatible external tool provider wiring.
surfaceTaxonomy.command_cli: Slash commands, CLI commands, prompt files, or invokable command templates.
surfaceTaxonomy.memory_context: Persistent memory, context retrieval, docs search, or codebase context features.
surfaceTaxonomy.permission_safety: Approval, sandbox, tool permission, auto-approve, deny, or file-access restriction controls.

requiredForRuntimeProjection: official docs or local runtime probe evidence

products.label: Claude Code

evidence.claim: Claude is a supported and default sync target.
Claude has a runtime profile and projection layout.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection.
products.nextAction: Maintain by changing canonical sources and running meta:sync plus meta:check.

products.label: Codex

evidence.claim: Codex is a supported and default sync target.
Codex has a runtime profile and projection layout.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection.
products.nextAction: Preserve .agents/skills as the project skill root and keep Codex hook validation current.

products.label: OpenClaw

evidence.claim: OpenClaw is a supported formal sync target; it is not selected by the primary default.
OpenClaw is a formal projection; stricter tool-denial behavior must be backed by OpenClaw-side strict tests, and can merge only after the evidence passes review.
OpenClaw workspace bootstrap injects AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, and BOOTSTRAP.md from the active workspace.
OpenClaw internal hooks cover command/lifecycle automation; blocking/canceling policy belongs to typed plugin hooks.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim projection with OpenClaw-native workspace, skill, config, and hook surfaces.
products.nextAction: Community PRs that improve OpenClaw behavior are welcome; contributors must include strict OpenClaw self-test evidence, especially for typed plugin tool-denial behavior, and changes can merge only after the evidence passes review.

products.label: Cursor

evidence.claim: Cursor is a supported formal sync target; it is not selected by the primary default.
Cursor has a runtime profile and projection layout.
Cursor project subagents are markdown files under .cursor/agents with YAML frontmatter.
Cursor hooks include preToolUse/postToolUse and failClosed for security-critical hooks.
Cursor project rules use .cursor/rules .mdc files and AGENTS.md guidance.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection with official subagent/rule/hook/MCP surfaces.
products.nextAction: Community PRs that improve Cursor behavior are welcome; contributors must include strict Cursor self-test evidence, especially for native choice-surface claims, and changes can merge only after the evidence passes review.

products.label: OpenCode

evidence.claim: ECC targets OpenCode through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists opencode as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an install target only; do not treat as a formal Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Add a formal profile and sync tests only if Meta_Kim owns an OpenCode projection.

products.label: Qwen Code

evidence.claim: ECC targets Qwen through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists qwen as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; generic runtime compatibility still needs a probe.
products.nextAction: Probe Qwen skill, agent, hook, and MCP surfaces before promotion.

products.label: Zed

evidence.claim: ECC targets Zed through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists zed as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; do not promote without a Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Probe Zed runtime surfaces before any sync manifest change.

products.label: Gemini CLI

evidence.claim: ECC targets Gemini through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists gemini as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; generic runtime compatibility still needs a probe.
products.nextAction: Probe Gemini runtime surfaces before any formal promotion.

evidence.claim: ECC targets CodeBuddy through its upstream CLI installer.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; do not promote without a Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Probe CodeBuddy runtime surfaces before any sync manifest change.

evidence.claim: ECC targets Antigravity through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists antigravity as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; do not promote without a Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Probe Antigravity runtime surfaces before any sync manifest change.

products.label: JoyCode

evidence.claim: ECC targets JoyCode through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists joycode as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; do not promote without a Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Probe JoyCode runtime surfaces before any sync manifest change.

products.label: Trae

genericCompatibility.skillPath: Trae Skills / SKILL.md-compatible skill packages; exact project and global paths need probe
genericCompatibility.agentPath: Trae custom agents in IDE settings; exact exported file path needs probe
genericCompatibility.mcpConfig: Trae MCP settings / Add MCP servers UI; exact file path needs probe

evidence.claim: Trae documents custom agents with prompts, MCP servers, built-in tools, and task-specific configuration.
Trae documents rules as persistent behavioral guidance.
Trae documents skills as task procedures that can be combined with MCP servers.
Trae documents MCP server integration for external tools and services.
Trae documents memory for persisted preferences and context.

products.decision: Admit Trae as candidate_probe only: official docs expose compatible primitives, but Meta_Kim lacks profile/layout/sync validation and local runtime probe evidence.
products.nextAction: Design a Trae adapter only after probing actual project/global paths, export format, MCP config shape, and validation hooks.

products.label: Kiro

genericCompatibility.skillPath: Kiro skills or powers packages; exact install path needs probe
genericCompatibility.hookConfig: .kiro/hooks/ or IDE Agent Hooks; exact serialized shape needs probe
genericCompatibility.mcpConfig: Kiro MCP Servers / powers MCP configuration; exact file path needs probe

evidence.claim: Kiro documents workspace and global steering files plus AGENTS.md support.
Kiro documents specs as structured planning artifacts.
Kiro documents Agent Hooks for IDE events, tool events, prompt submission, and shell commands.
Kiro documents skills as portable open-standard packages.
Kiro documents powers as bundles of POWER.md, MCP server configuration, steering, and hooks.

products.decision: Admit Kiro as candidate_probe only: steering, specs, hooks, skills, powers, and MCP are compatible primitives, not a Meta_Kim runtime projection.
products.nextAction: Probe .kiro artifact layouts, power packaging, CLI behavior, and hook denial semantics before promotion.

products.label: Windsurf / Devin Desktop Cascade

genericCompatibility.skillPath: .devin/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md or legacy Windsurf skill locations; exact current path needs probe
genericCompatibility.hookConfig: Cascade Hooks; exact project/global file path needs probe
genericCompatibility.mcpConfig: Cascade MCP configuration; exact file path needs probe

evidence.claim: Cascade documents skills as SKILL.md workflow bundles with progressive disclosure.
Cascade documents memories, rules, workflows, skills, and AGENTS.md-style customization surfaces.
Cascade documents MCP integration and remote/local MCP server configuration.
Cascade documents pre/post hooks for logging, validation, guardrails, and governance.
Cascade documents AGENTS.md as location-scoped instructions feeding the rules engine.

products.decision: Admit Windsurf/Devin Desktop Cascade as candidate_probe only: Cascade has multiple compatible primitives, but current Meta_Kim has no formal profile, layout, or live acceptance for it.
products.nextAction: Probe current Devin Desktop and legacy Windsurf folder names, skill priority, hook behavior, and MCP config before any adapter.

products.label: Cline

genericCompatibility.skillPath: .cline/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md; ~/.cline/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md; legacy .clinerules/skills and .claude/skills imports are documented by Cline
genericCompatibility.mcpConfig: ~/.cline/mcp.json or IDE MCP settings; project-level behavior needs probe

evidence.claim: Cline documents experimental SKILL.md-based skills with workspace and global storage locations including .cline/skills and ~/.cline/skills.
Cline documents workspace and global rules, AGENTS.md compatibility, and conditional rule activation.
Cline documents MCP server configuration, CLI MCP wizard, autoApprove, and tool approval guidance.

products.decision: Admit Cline as candidate_probe only: rules, skills, CLI, and MCP are compatible primitives, but agents/hooks projection is not proven for Meta_Kim.
products.nextAction: Probe Cline skill precedence, CLI and extension project config behavior, and hook/plugin surfaces before designing a runtime adapter.

products.label: Roo Code

evidence.claim: Roo Code documents project/global custom modes with tool groups and mode-specific instructions.
Roo Code documents SKILL.md-based skills under .roo, ~/.roo, and .agents skill roots.
Roo Code documents workspace/global rule directories and AGENTS.md integration.
Roo Code documents project .roo/mcp.json and global MCP settings.
Roo Code documents project/global markdown slash commands.

products.decision: Admit Roo Code as candidate_probe only: it is highly compatible at the primitive level, but Meta_Kim has no Roo runtime projection or live validation yet.
products.nextAction: Probe Roo mode, skill, MCP, command, and permission behavior before considering a formal projection.

products.label: Continue

genericCompatibility.agentPath: config.yaml assistant definitions; exact Meta_Kim agent projection needs design

evidence.claim: Continue documents local and global rules under .continue/rules and ~/.continue/rules.
Continue documents MCP servers under .continue/mcpServers and config mcpServers.
Continue documents invokable prompts as slash commands in Chat, Plan, and Agent modes.
Continue config.yaml defines agents with models, rules, tools, prompts, context, and MCP servers.
Continue CLI supports slash commands, MCP tools, and tool permission flags.

products.decision: Admit Continue as candidate_probe only: rules, config agents, MCP, prompts, and CLI permissions are compatible primitives, not a Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Probe Continue config.yaml projection, local rule precedence, MCP block files, prompt commands, and CLI permission semantics before promotion.

products.label: Qoder CLI

evidence.claim: Open issue requests qodercli compatibility.
Qoder skills are folders containing SKILL.md under user or project .qoder/skills.
Qoder subagents use markdown files under user or project .qoder/agents.
Qoder hooks are configured in settings.json and can block via exit code 2 for supported events.
Qoder MCP servers can be configured with qodercli mcp add or .mcp.json/project settings.
ECC install help does not list qoder as a supported target.

products.decision: Admit Qoder as candidate_probe only: official docs prove compatible primitives, but Meta_Kim lacks profile/layout/sync validation and ECC currently omits qoder.
products.nextAction: Use a generic adapter probe first; promote only after adding a profile, projection layout, generated target paths, sync tests, and install policy.
